title: Subject Pronouns
intro: Subject pronouns tell you who is doing the action. Spanish uses them less than English because the verb ending already shows who is speaking.

sections.title: Spanish often drops the pronoun!
sections.content:
Because the verb ending already tells you who is doing the action, Spanish speakers often skip the subject pronoun:
• Hablo español. (I speak Spanish.) — no 'yo' needed!
• ¿Hablas inglés? (Do you speak English?) — no 'tú' needed!
You'll use the pronoun for emphasis or clarity: Yo hablo inglés, ¿y tú?

sections.title: tú vs usted — how formal are you?
sections.content:
• tú — for friends, family, children, and peers your age
• usted — for strangers, older people, bosses, and formal situations
In doubt, use usted — it's never rude!

sections.label: 🌍 Spain vs Latin America
sections.content:
This is the biggest real difference:
• In Spain: vosotros / vosotras for 'you all' (informal)
• In Latin America: ustedes for ALL 'you all' (formal and informal)
If you use 'ustedes' everywhere, you'll always be understood.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'I' in Spanish?
Your boss asks you a question. You address them as:
questions.explanation: Use 'usted' for formal situations — bosses, strangers, older people ✓

questions.prompt: In Latin America, 'you all' (plural you) is always:
questions.explanation: In Latin America, 'ustedes' is used for both formal and informal 'you all' ✓

questions.prompt: In Spain, the informal 'you all' is:
questions.explanation: In Spain, 'vosotros' is the informal plural 'you' — unique to Spain! ✓

questions.prompt: 'Hablo español' — the 'yo' (I) is missing. Is this correct Spanish?
options: No — you must always include the pronoun
Yes — Spanish often drops the subject pronoun
questions.explanation: Spanish is a 'pro-drop' language — the verb ending -o shows it's 'yo', so the pronoun is optional ✓

questions.prompt: How do you say 'she' in Spanish?
questions.explanation: 'she' = ella ✓ (él = he, ellos = they masc., usted = formal you)

questions.prompt: How do you say 'we' in Spanish?
questions.explanation: 'we' = nosotros (masc. or mixed group) / nosotras (all female) ✓
